papers.title: Uneven Evolution of Cognition Across Generations of Generative AI Models
papers.reason: Excluded because it evaluates the cognitive abilities of generative AI models rather than human participants.

papers.title: ASR-Agnostic Multimodal Spectrotemporal Modeling for Early Dementia Detection
papers.reason: It develops a human speech-based dementia biomarker, but lacks an interactive decision task, eye tracking, qualifying EEG analysis, and a cognitive process model.

papers.title: Demystifying Video Reasoning
papers.reason: Excluded because it studies reasoning and working memory inside diffusion-based video generation models rather than human cognition.

papers.title: The Richest Paradigm You're Not Using: Commercial Videogames at the Intersection of Human-Computer Interaction and Cognitive Science
papers.reason: It centrally proposes commercial videogames as interactive human executive-function paradigms measured through behavioral timing and eye tracking, although it is methodological rather than an empirical study.

papers.title: Pupillometry and Brain Dynamics for Cognitive Load in Working Memory
papers.reason: It analyzes pupillometry and EEG during a sequential digit-span task, but the described EEG analysis uses generic time-series features rather than frequency-domain or connectivity methods.

papers.title: Quant VideoGen: Auto-Regressive Long Video Generation via 2-Bit KV-Cache Quantization
papers.reason: Excluded because it concerns video generation and KV-cache memory in machine learning models rather than human participants.

papers.title: Cognitive Load Estimation Using Brain Foundation Models and Interpretability for BCIs
papers.reason: It addresses continuous human EEG-based cognitive-load monitoring, but provides no interactive task, eye tracking, qualifying spectral or connectivity analysis, or cognitive generative model.

papers.title: Cross-Modal Computational Model of Brain-Heart Interactions via HRV and EEG Feature
papers.reason: It studies human workload monitoring with HRV and EEG, but EEG is limited to band-power and generic features while XGBoost and synthetic HRV generation are not cognitive decision-process models.

papers.title: Unveiling the Heart-Brain Connection: An Analysis of ECG in Cognitive Performance
papers.reason: It assesses human cognitive workload through ECG-to-EEG feature prediction, but only EEG spectral summaries are described and no qualifying task, eye tracking, or cognitive process model is central.

papers.title: Your Eyes Controlled the Game: Real-Time Cognitive Training Adaptation based on Eye-Tracking and Physiological Data in Virtual Reality
papers.reason: It combines interactive adaptive VR attention and working-memory tasks with central eye-tracking analysis for real-time cognitive-load assessment in human participants.

papers.title: Uneven Evolution of Cognition Across Generations of Generative AI Models
papers.reason: Excluded because it psychometrically evaluates the cognitive abilities of generative AI models rather than human participants.

papers.title: ASR-Agnostic Multimodal Spectrotemporal Modeling for Early Dementia Detection
papers.reason: The paper addresses human dementia detection from elicited speech but lacks an interactive decision task, eye tracking, qualifying EEG analysis, or a cognitive process model.

papers.title: Demystifying Video Reasoning
papers.reason: Excluded because it studies reasoning and working memory mechanisms inside diffusion-based video generation models.

papers.title: The Richest Paradigm You're Not Using: Commercial Videogames at the Intersection of Human-Computer Interaction and Cognitive Science
papers.reason: Its proposed methodology centrally combines interactive commercial-game behavior with eye tracking and behavioral timing, although it is a perspective rather than an empirical validation study.

papers.title: Pupillometry and Brain Dynamics for Cognitive Load in Working Memory
papers.reason: Human digit-span performance and pupillometry are central, while the EEG analysis uses generic Catch-22 features rather than specified frequency-domain or connectivity measures.

papers.title: Cognitive Load Estimation Using Brain Foundation Models and Interpretability for BCIs
papers.reason: It concerns human EEG-based cognitive-load monitoring, but the summary provides no interactive paradigm or qualifying frequency-domain or connectivity analysis.

papers.title: Cross-Modal Computational Model of Brain-Heart Interactions via HRV and EEG Feature
papers.reason: The study monitors human workload using ECG-to-EEG regression, but EEG is represented mainly by band-power and generic features and the synthetic generator is not a cognitive decision-process model.

papers.title: Unveiling the Heart-Brain Connection: An Analysis of ECG in Cognitive Performance
papers.reason: It targets human cognitive monitoring through ECG projections onto EEG features, but reports only spectral and generic features without qualifying connectivity or an analyzed interactive behavioral paradigm.

papers.title: Your Eyes Controlled the Game: Real-Time Cognitive Training Adaptation based on Eye-Tracking and Physiological Data in Virtual Reality
papers.reason: The study centrally analyzes eye-tracking data during adaptive interactive VR tasks targeting sustained attention, working memory, and cognitive load.

papers.title: How Light Shapes Memory: Beta Synchrony in the Temporal-Parietal Cortex Predicts Cognitive Ergonomics for BCI Applications
papers.reason: It combines interactive recall and sequence working-memory tasks with central analysis of beta-phase EEG connectivity across cortical regions.

papers.title: Setting up for failure: automatic discovery of the neural mechanisms of cognitive errors
papers.reason: Excluded because its neural validation concerns macaque data rather than an exclusively human participant population.

papers.title: A Quantitative Framework for Assessing Sleep Quality from EEG Time Series in Complex Dynamic Systems
papers.reason: The methodology includes a working-memory task and central EEG phase-amplitude coupling and connectivity analyses, although sleep-quality classification is the primary objective.

papers.title: Quantifying the Effects of Word Length, Frequency, and Predictability on Dyslexia
papers.reason: Human eye tracking is central to the dyslexia analysis, but naturalistic reading is not an interactive timed or sequential decision paradigm.

papers.title: Working Memory Functional Connectivity Analysis for Dementia Classification using EEG
papers.reason: It combines staged working-memory tasks with detailed phase-lag and entropy-based EEG functional-connectivity analysis for AD, MCI, and control classification.

papers.title: Bayesian Distributional Models of Executive Functioning
papers.reason: Excluded because the evaluation uses simulated sessions and synthetic test items without a human participant study or validation on real human data.

papers.title: Bibliometric-enhanced Systematic Literature Review of EEG in Education: Learning Concepts, Computational Methods, and Research Opportunities
papers.reason: This review concerns EEG-based assessment of human attention and learning but does not itself implement any of the four methodological elements.

papers.title: EEG-Based Framework for Reflexive and Perceptual Assessment in CLIS: Preliminary Study in Healthy Volunteers
papers.reason: The healthy-volunteer study centrally uses frequency-specific steady-state auditory and visual EEG responses, but its paradigms are passive rather than behaviorally interactive.

papers.title: EEG-Based Cognitive Load Classification During Landmark-Based VR Navigation
papers.reason: Participants perform interactive landmark-based VR navigation, but the EEG methodology reports classifier performance without specified frequency-domain or connectivity analysis.

papers.title: A Unified Cortical Circuit Model with Divisive Normalization and Self-Excitation for Robust Representation and Memory Maintenance
papers.reason: Excluded because it is a theoretical neural-circuit simulation without a human participant population or validation on human data.

papers.title: Dissociating Cognitive Load and Stress Responses Using Single-Channel EEG: Behavioral and Neural Correlates of Anxiety Across Cognitive States
papers.reason: It assesses human cognitive load and stress using single-channel EEG, but the summary describes band-level features and auditory exposure without a qualifying interactive decision task or connectivity analysis.

papers.title: Sleep Brain and Cardiac Activity Predict Cognitive Flexibility and Conceptual Reasoning Using Deep Learning
papers.reason: The paper predicts human executive-function scores from passive sleep EEG and ECG using deep learning, with only EEG power bands and no cognitive process model.

papers.title: Evaluating Cognitive Assessment Tools:A Comparative Analysis of MMSE, RUDAS, SAGE, ADAS and MoCA for Early Dementia Detection
papers.reason: The review is relevant to early human dementia assessment but contains no interactive digital paradigm, eye tracking, qualifying EEG analysis, or computational cognitive model.

papers.title: Early Detection of Cognitive Impairment in Elderly using a Passive FPVS-EEG BCI and Machine Learning -- Extended Version
papers.reason: It directly targets early cognitive-impairment detection with human EEG, but the paradigm is explicitly passive and the summary gives no qualifying frequency-domain or connectivity method.

papers.title: Immersive Virtual Reality Assessments of Working Memory and Psychomotor Skills: A Comparison between Immersive and Non-Immersive Assessments
papers.reason: Human participants complete interactive sequential working-memory and reaction-time tasks in VR and on a PC, but no eye tracking, EEG, or cognitive process model is included.

papers.title: Triangulating LLM Progress through Benchmarks, Games, and Cognitive Tests
papers.reason: Excluded because it evaluates LLM abilities using benchmarks, games, and cognitive tests rather than studying human cognition.

papers.title: Interactive Virtual Games: Winners for Deep Cognitive Assessment
papers.reason: The clinical human study centrally combines interactive executive-function minigames with continuous movement and gaze-trajectory analysis for individualized cognitive assessment.

papers.title: Mazed and Confused: A Dataset of Cybersickness, Working Memory, Mental Load, Physical Load, and Attention During a Real Walking Task in VR
papers.reason: Participants interactively navigate VR mazes while performing attention and working-memory tasks, with eye tracking analyzed alongside behavioral and physiological measures.

papers.title: Pathformer3D: A 3D Scanpath Transformer for 360° Images
papers.reason: The model is evaluated on human eye-tracking scanpath datasets, but the viewing condition is passive and the Transformer is not a generative cognitive decision-process model.

papers.title: Transcranial low-level laser stimulation in near infrared-II region for brain safety and protection
papers.reason: The human safety study co-records EEG and executive-function assessments, but neither an interactive task nor frequency-domain or connectivity EEG analysis is methodologically detailed.

papers.reason: Excluded because it psychometrically evaluates the cognitive abilities of generative AI models rather than human participants.
